text.title: ops-partnership-strategy Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ops-partnership-strategy as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Models confidently invent partnership-team contact names and email addresses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always validate contacts via LinkedIn or company About page before any outreach. Never send to an email the agent generated without verification — sending to fabricated addresses creates CAN-SPAM and spam-filter issues.

failure-mode.description: Cold-outreach output drifts toward spammy templates with superlatives despite constraints.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add hard constraints: "must reference [X]-specific recent action", "no superlatives", "under 150 words". Review every outreach before send regardless of constraint specification.

failure-mode.description: Agent asserts audience overlap without citing the source data point.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require the agent to quote a specific number and source ("their pricing page says 8,000 customers"). Asserted overlap with no data source will be challenged by the partner immediately.

failure-mode.description: Agent auto-generates revenue-share math without showing the formula step by step.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs flub multi-step financial calculations. Require explicit formula output ("30% of $99 MRR = $29.70/month x 12 = $356.40/year per referral") before any proposal is drafted.

failure-mode.description: Agent drafts contract terms or legal summaries without human review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Partnership agreements that include integration rights, IP licensing, or data-sharing require legal review — especially if enterprise deals will reference the agreement for DPA/SCC compliance.
